meta.title: Conjunto Monumental de la Alcazaba de Almería
meta.description: Complete travel guide to the Alcazaba of Almería. Learn about this iconic Moorish fortress in Andalusia, Spain, including location and visitor information.

intro.title: Discover the Alcazaba
intro.description: The Conjunto Monumental de la Alcazaba de Almería is one of the largest surviving Moorish fortresses in Spain. Located in Almería, Andalusia, this magnificent monumental complex boasts a rich history and overlooks the city and the Mediterranean Sea, offering an immersive historical and cultural experience.

visitGuide.title: Visit Tips
items: Wear comfortable walking shoes as the fortress has many cobblestone paths and stairs.
During summer, protect yourself from the sun and visit early morning or late afternoon to avoid the heat.
The fortress provides excellent panoramic views of the city of Almería and its port.

sections.title: Historical Background
sections.content: The Alcazaba was ordered to be built in the 10th century by Abd al-Rahman III, the ruler of Al-Andalus. It was initially not only a military fortress but also the local political and administrative center, witnessing Almería's prosperity during Islamic rule.

sections.title: Architecture & Layout
sections.content: The castle is divided into three main enclosures: the first enclosure was once a civilian residential area and is now a beautiful garden; the second enclosure was the historical core, containing ruins of palaces, baths, and mosques; the third enclosure is a defensive fortress added by the Catholic Monarchs after the Reconquista.

sections.title: Cultural Significance
sections.content: As an important cultural heritage of Andalusia (BIC), the Alcazaba blends Islamic and Christian architectural styles. It is not only a symbol of the local area but has also served as a filming location for famous movies and TV series (such as Game of Thrones).

sections.title: Archaeology & Preservation
sections.content: This is a key protected historical heritage of the Andalusian region. Successive archaeological excavations continue to reveal the rich historical strata of this complex, and the government carries out ongoing restoration projects to protect these precious monuments.

basicInfo.title: Basic Information
basicInfo.officialNameValue: Conjunto Monumental de la Alcazaba de Almería
basicInfo.addressValue: RGRH+6W, C. Almanzor, s/n, 04002 Almería, Spain

hours.title: Visiting Hours
hours.parkTime: Open Tuesday to Sunday (hours vary by season, closed on Mondays)
hours.bestTimeSpring: Spring: Pleasant weather, gardens in bloom
hours.bestTimeSummer: Summer: Cooler in the mornings and evenings, avoid midday heat
hours.bestTimeAutumn: Autumn: Mild weather, perfect for leisurely walks
hours.bestTimeWinter: Winter: Fewer tourists, quiet appreciation of the monuments
hours.tip: Be sure to check the official website or contact officials for the latest opening hours and holiday schedules before your trip.

tickets.title: Admission Information
tickets.parkPrice: Free for EU citizens, other visitors typically pay a nominal fee (around 1.5 EUR)
tickets.guidedPrice: Official or third-party paid guided tours available

transport.title: How to Get Here
transport.fromCenterDesc: The fortress is located above the old town, within walking distance from the city center, though it involves an uphill climb.
transport.fromStationDesc: About 2 km from Almería Central Station, accessible by municipal bus or taxi.
transport.fromAirportDesc: About 10 km from Almería Airport, it is recommended to take a taxi or airport bus to the city center and transfer.
transport.publicTransportDesc: Several local bus lines stop near the fortress.
transport.walkingDesc: Walk through the historical Medina alleys for unique scenery.
transport.drivingDesc: Streets around the fortress are narrow; it is recommended to park in public lots in the city and walk.

route.title: Recommended Route
route.overview: Journey through the three enclosures and experience the evolution of history.
steps: Enter through the main gate and walk through the beautiful gardens of the first enclosure
Enter the second enclosure to explore the palace ruins and Islamic cisterns
Climb the Muro de la Vela for a panoramic view of the city
Enter the third enclosure to see the solid fortress built during the Catholic Monarchs' era
Enjoy the view of the harbor and the Mediterranean Sea from the north wall viewpoint

spots.title: First Enclosure Gardens
spots.description: A perfect background featuring Islamic-style pools and lush vegetation.

spots.title: Muro de la Vela Viewpoint
spots.description: From here, capture Almería's iconic white walls and the blue Mediterranean Sea.
spots.location: Between the Second and Third Enclosures

spots.title: Third Enclosure Main Tower
spots.description: The magnificent Christian-style tower showcases the spectacular military defenses.

spots.title: Ancient Wall Ruins
spots.description: Capture the weight of history and magnificent sunsets along the winding ancient walls.

hotels.name: Almería Old Town
hotels.desc: Staying in the old town allows for easy walking access to the fortress, surrounded by authentic Tapas bars.

hotels.name: Near Paseo Marítimo
hotels.desc: If you love sea views and beaches, this is an ideal choice, just a short drive from the fortress.

hotels.name: City Center Commercial District
hotels.desc: Offers modern hotel facilities and convenient transport, perfect for shopping and urban exploration.

reviews.title: Visitor Reviews
reviews.declaration: Reviews can be viewed via Google Maps.
reviews.moreReviews: View more reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Carlos M.
items.text: A spectacular historical monument, very well preserved. The views of Almería from the top are unbeatable.

items.name: Laura G.
items.text: The gardens in the first enclosure are stunning; the water features and plants are very calming. Absolutely worth a visit!

items.name: David R.
items.text: Deep historical significance. The climb is a bit tiring, but seeing the third enclosure's fortress and the sea makes it all worthwhile.

items.name: Sarah W.
items.text: One of the most magnificent Moorish castles I've seen in Andalusia. Not as crowded as Granada, making for a great experience.

items.name: Antonio V.
items.text: Highly recommended for history and architecture lovers. The fact that Game of Thrones was filmed here is also a fun highlight.

items.name: Elena P.
items.text: Remember to bring water if visiting in summer. The views are breathtaking, and the whole complex makes you feel the prosperity of a thousand years ago.

officialManagement.title: About the Alcazaba
officialManagement.text: The Conjunto Monumental de la Alcazaba de Almería is an important monument directly administered by the region of Andalusia, Spain. This cultural heritage is strictly protected and managed daily by the Regional Government of Andalusia and local historical heritage agencies.
